text: I am here.
I admit, when your people contacted me, I was not sure what to expect.
Invitation. To leave my motherland and sit in remote room and wait in front of metal box.
Is not something I do often, Mr. President.
But here I am, waiting.
For you. Now, You have my attention.
I suggest you do not waste it.
Thank me. Yes. You are very welcome.
So gracious of you to thank me. Very polite.
America is always very polite, yes? On surface that is.
I find it is places underneath surface that are more interesting.
But no need for chit-chat.
You called this meeting, Mr. President.
What are we discussing today.
Business. Of course.
Americans are always in hurry. Always rushing.
To meetings. To conclusions.
But yes. Business.
What do you want from me, Mr. President?
You want peace, then.
I agree. These days, no country ever gets 'peace'.
They get no peace from the filth of Western influence.
You say you want peace. Tell me what that means to you, Mr. President.
Ha. Our differences.
What you say is so very vague, Mr. President.
ideological ones? territorial ones?
Or perhaps more recent ones.
You mean to talk about wars you start in name of capitalism.
Of people you and your business associates kill for money?
Your American traditions is different from ours, I must say.

links.label: What we do is none of your business
I want to discuss a path forward.

text: Discuss my aggression?
You invite me to talk, and first thing you want to talk about is *my* aggression.
Is very bold opening, Mr. President.
Your boldness come from fact that we are not here face to face, yes?
Why must you hide? Why must you have me here, away from motherland?
Alright. If you seek such diplomacy, then I can consider compromising.
What are you prepared to offer Russia in return for real peace?
Mutual disarmament. You are serious?
Mr. President, I do not know if this is brave or naive.
It is hard to tell, from metal box. Perhaps this was plan all along.
Russia does not disarm based on request.
Not from neighbor, ally, and certainly not from you.
Whose country has been producing and using new weapons at record pace.
I have seen contracts, Mr. President.
Your defense industry is very busy right now.
So you must understand the need for our arms.
And you will forgive me if I find word disarmament little ironic.
Your business. Very American to talk of business.
I apologize, but it is very much my business.
I cannot stand idly while West swallows nations in name of freedom.
You and your allies are aggressors in world, obstacles to peace.
It is only reasonable why I must do what I do. Defend, and take before you take.

links.label: Then let's discuss a path forward.

text: Defensive.
Every country says this, yes?
My weapons are defensive. Your troops on my borders are defensive.
At some point, Mr. President, everything is defensive
I am not interested in your empty word.
I am interested in action.
What are you willing to do?
We do what we must, Mr. President. I am not blind to it.
But I am also not blind to atrocities you commit. Same ones you accuse me of.
Good people, wanting better leaders, reduced to ash by your military.
but please, lecture me about what I'm doing wrong.
Are you here to scold me, or for something else? Tell me now, Mr. President.
You would pull back your forces.
This is significant, if true.
Only if true. I have heard these promises before, Mr. President.
Promises are very light. They do not weigh much.
What makes this one different from others?

links.label: I will put it in writing.
You have my word as President.

text: Lifted sanctions.
Hmm.
You think this is what Russia wants most.
Money. Trade. Access to your markets.
Perhaps you are right. Perhaps this is practical.
But I want you to understand something, Mr. President.
Russia has survived your sanctions.
We have adapted. It was not comfortable, but we are still here.
You are still in your wherever you are.
And we are still standing.
But it is small one.
What else are you offering?

links.label: A withdrawal of forces as well.
That is all I can offer today.

text: You will pause contracts.
Will the men who hold those contracts will allow this?
In my experience, men who profit from war do not stop when asked nicely.
I do not believe you have the authority.

links.label: I have the authority. Let's talk.

text: Not how your administration operates.
I see.
Then I must have misread your business contracts.
And lobbying disclosures.
And news reports.
Perhaps my intelligence is wrong.
Is this why you call meeting? State your claim.

links.label: I am fully aware of my administration.
I want to discuss a path forward.

text: Do you truly think that I would believe you?
To accept your terms would mean agreeing to be betrayed later on.
Russia will not be led into such trap.
This, Mr. President, is poor excuse of trap.

links.label: Not a trap. We want peace

text: Your word.
As President.
Mr. President, Your word is not an official statement
I like to believe in best of people. I think you are good person.
But presidents change. Administrations change.
Your fragile presidency would never guarantee my land's propserity.
Nor would future presidencies. Whatever peace you suggest, it's not worth my time.
This is a sorry excuse for a negotiation, Mr. President
Is that so?
Then we have not made very much progress today.
My goal of this first conversation of ours, Mr. President,
was to take measure of you.
To understand what you were willing to do and what you were not.
I have taken your measure, Mr. President.
I find that you are weak.
Russia will be patient for better oppurtunity in future.
We have been patient for very long time.
And we will be patient little longer.
Goodbye, Mr. President.
Then you already know what I know.
Yet we are here arguing.
Tell me, president. What is it that you want?

links.label: I want a signed peace agreement.
I want a ceasefire, for now.

text: I am here.
I admit, when your people contact me, I am not sure what to expect.
Invitation. To leave my motherland and sit in remote room and wait in front of metal box.
Is not something I do often, Mr. President.
But here I am, waiting.
For you. Now, You have my attention.
I suggest you do not waste it.
Thank me. Yes. You are very welcome.
So gracious of you to thank me. Very polite.
America is always very polite, yes? On surface that is.
I find it is places underneath surface that are more interesting.
But no need for chit-chat.
You called this meeting, Mr. President.
What are we discussing today.
You want peace, then.
I agree. These days, no country ever gets 'peace'.
You say you want peace. Tell me what that means to you, Mr. President.
What? I --
I admit, it is rare to see such leader extending helping hand.
But we do not need help. Motherland is thriving, and will continue to do so.
That said, what are you even offering to say such boldness?
Ha! You are dreamer, then, yes?
I respect that. Leader with vision make it far.
You are strong for believing such. I also seek a prosperous world.

links.label: Helping your country will make it happen

text: Mutual disarmament. You are serious?
Mr. President, I do not know if this is brave or naive.
It is hard to tell, from metal box. Perhaps this was plan all along.
Russia does not disarm based on request.
Not from neighbor, ally, and certainly not from you.
Whose country has been producing and using new weapons at record pace.
I have seen contracts, Mr. President.
Your defense industry is very busy right now.
So you must understand the need for our arms.
And you will forgive me if I find word disarmament little ironic.

links.label: Those contracts are purely for defense!

text: Indeed.
Then, what do you propose, Mr. President?
Ha! Do not pollute our land with your Western technology.
And I will never let your slimy businessmen into our country.
It was an insult to suggest such matters.
And it was a mistake to come here.
I'm leaving.
Are you suggesting economic aid?
I must remind you, Mr. President, that our struggle, be that as they may,
are direct result from you and your allies interference.
Do not offer solution after giving problem.
Hm...
I can agree to those terms, Mr. President.
Russia will, likewise, respond in kind.
Yes, for a more prosperous world, this would be best.
Is there anything else you wanted?
What? I do not believe you.
To be rid of nuclear weapons will invite trouble in the future, Mr. President.
I cannot, in faith, trust you either.
How could I trust you, after everything I know of America's history?
To give such blind faith in someone, a opponent, no less,
I do not understand you.
Your compassion is something I left behind, long ago, Mr. President.
My years as Russia's leader have made it as such.
But, if you trust me, then I will trust you.
We can start with the removal of nukes, then the lifting of economic sanctions.
I am ready for peace.
To give such blind faith in someone, a opponent, no less,
I do not understand you.
Your compassion is something I left behind, long ago, Mr. President.
My years as Russia's leader have made it as such.
But, if you trust me, then I will trust you.
We can start with the removal of nukes, then the lifting of economic sanctions.
I am ready for peace.
I am here.
I admit, when your people contact me, I am not sure what to expect.
Invitation. To leave my motherland and sit in remote room and wait in front of metal box.
Is not something I do often, Mr. President.
But here I am, waiting.
For you. Now, You have my attention.
I suggest you do not waste it.
Excuse me?
I believe I did not hear you quite right, Mr. President.
A country does not simply, 'surrender', without a conflict.
You should choose your words more carefully now.
I call my commanding military officers, and your country is ash.
You are bluffing, and doing bad job, Mr. President
Your country may be inferior, but it is not dumb.
To give maniac like you all power over nuclear bombs.

links.label: We called you to a remote location

text: Yes, you Americans insisted for such.
I was hoping for talks of diplomacy.
I ensured that there was threat to me, that nothing was suspicious.
But now, I realize that this was part of plan.
Why did you call me hear, then?
What?
You, you crazy man!
Who let you in charge?!
You disgust me, you horrid human being.
I'm leaving. Consider this war.
State your terms, mad man.
I despise you.
I should have never come here.
I can't. No, I absolutely cannot.
No, no, you're still bluffing, yes?
This must be bluff... !@#!$!, it is not.
I, agree to your terms, president.
What is to happen next?
Alright.
But you are a coward, hiding in a bunker while threatening annihilation.
Your presidency is fragile, fleeting.
My rule is not.
I will remember your insolence, for the next man foolish to threaten me.
This is not over, American.
We are done.
How very interesting that you decide Russia's position in the world.
Tell me, Mr. President.
What exactly is it that is not tenable?
That we exist? That we have borders? That we do not simply agree with everything you say?

links.label: Withdraw your forces or we'll make you.

text: Your military provocation are not tolerated, either.
We do what we must to defend against your Western threats.
If you seek such issues with that, first disarm your military.
Then we can talk. Until then, we will continue to be in conflict.

links.label: I will give you 24 hours to comply.

text: Mr. President.
I want you to hear yourself.
Do you understand what you are saying?
No one has made Russia do anything in three hundred years.
It has stood tall before you presidency, and will continue afterwards
But please, you are welcome to try.
And I will respond by nuking yours.
Mutually assured destruction, yes?
I think you are smart enough to know why your threat is empty.
One call, and your country is ash.
Of course you overstepped.
Now, can we talk diplomatically?
Unless this was the only reason you called me here.
To issue empty threats is so detracting of you, Mr. President.
Peace after your poor bluffing?
Fine. Let us talk 'peace'.
I'm surprised you know meaning of that word, Mr. President.
What is it that you have in mind?
And so it shall be.
I thank you, Mr. President, for allowing us to truly see who is the superior.
I hope you, your soldiers, and your cities are ready for what is coming.
Russia officially declares war on the United States and their allies.
Ha! Twenty-four hours?
Russia need none, we have been ready to fight since our revolution.
You threaten us, yet you hide like a coward in your American bunker.
So, I will entertain you. What happens in twenty-four hours? If we do not comply?
You threaten us, yet you hide like a coward in your American bunker.
So, I will entertain you. What will this military action entail?
You bastard!
How long have you been waiting for this!?
I can not believe this. I will be responding with my own missiles.
This is war. Mr. President.
